summary: Reviewed the supplied remediation spec excerpt against the review brief and project conventions in the pack. The largest risk is traceability: the pack has no diff or file contents and the spec is truncated after D1, so most of the promised 24-defect remediation cannot be verified. Within the visible spec, several requirements are sound, but there are material ambiguities around completed-booking edit behavior and Date-TBD handling.
appears_sound: D4 correctly targets the bypass at `updateBooking()` and requires sharing one `ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS` constant with `updateBookingStatus()` instead of duplicating transition logic in two paths (`tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:40`).

D12 includes a server-side immutability guard in `updateBooking()`, so completed/cancelled edit protection is not only enforced by the edit page redirect (`tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:103`).

D1 correctly proposes setting `hold_expiry` to `null` for Date-TBD bookings, which matches the intended cron-skip behavior for bookings without a real event date (`tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:171`).
D13's visible status guard blocks both `cancelled` and `completed` bookings while leaving draft/confirmed deposit recording allowed (`tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:140`).

findings.description: Traceability gap: the pack asks for verification of a 24-defect remediation, but provides no diff, no changed file contents, no related files, and a spec truncated after D1. Groups C-L, the migration, cron changes, permission changes, contract text, query fixes, and all code/spec matching are therefore unverifiable from the allowed input.
findings.evidence: The pack says `Changed Files _(none detected)_`, `Diff _(no diff output)_`, `Changed File Contents _(no files to include)_`, and `[spec truncated at line 200 — original has 953 lines]`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: This may have been intended as a review of only the visible spec excerpt rather than the whole remediation.
findings.what_would_confirm: Provide the full remediation spec plus the relevant changed file snapshots or diff for the listed key files.

findings.description: Unresolved ambiguity: D4 requires the completed-booking edit form to render with only `Completed`, while D12 requires completed and cancelled edit routes to redirect to the detail page. Implementers cannot satisfy both UI acceptance criteria at once without a priority decision.
findings.evidence: D4 acceptance says `Edit form for a completed booking shows only Completed`, but D12 later specifies redirecting when `booking.status === 'completed' || booking.status === 'cancelled'`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: D12 may intentionally supersede D4, but the spec does not state that the D4 acceptance criterion should be removed.
findings.what_would_confirm: Decide whether immutable bookings should redirect, show a read-only edit page, or show a status-only form, then update both acceptance sections consistently.

findings.description: Spec defect: the D1 prose says SMS should treat bookings as TBD when `date_tbd` is true or `internal_notes` contains `DATE_TBD_NOTE`, but the proposed code checks only `internal_notes`. A TBD booking without that note would still expose the fake stored event date in customer SMS.
findings.evidence: The requirement names both `date_tbd` and `internal_notes`, while the code sets `const isTbd = booking.internal_notes?.includes(DATE_TBD_NOTE);`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The `sendCreationSms()` booking shape may not currently include a `date_tbd` field.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect or specify the `sendCreationSms()` input shape and require selecting/passing `date_tbd` where available, with the note check only as a fallback.

findings.description: Spec defect: D1 says TBD bookings have no balance due date, but the proposed code preserves any supplied `input.balance_due_date`. That can attach a real payment deadline to a booking whose event date is deliberately fake.
findings.evidence: The visible fix says `no hold expiry, no balance due date`, but then sets `balanceDueDate = balanceDueDate || null` inside the TBD branch.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The product may allow staff-entered manual balance due dates for TBD bookings, but that exception is not documented.
findings.what_would_confirm: Clarify whether TBD bookings must always store `balance_due_date = null` or whether manual due dates are allowed, and add acceptance criteria for that case.

findings.description: Implementation defect risk: the proposed immutable-status guard treats every non-`status` key as an edit, rather than checking whether a field actually changed or is merely `undefined`/unchanged. If any caller sends a full form payload or no-op fields, valid status-only intents could be rejected unexpectedly.
findings.evidence: The guard uses `Object.keys(input).filter(k => k !== 'status')` and rejects when that array is non-empty.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If all callers pass sparse patch objects and status changes use `updateBookingStatus()`, the raw-key check may be sufficient.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect all `updateBooking()` callers and specify whether the guard should compare effective changes against `currentBooking` or ignore `undefined`/unchanged values.

findings.description: Trace gap: D13 changes only `finalizeDepositPaymentWithClient()`, but acceptance requires PayPal deposit capture on completed bookings to fail too. Without `payments.ts` or the PayPal capture/webhook route in the pack, there is no evidence that every PayPal path goes through this guarded helper.
findings.evidence: The fix names one function, while the acceptance criteria separately require both manual deposit recording and PayPal deposit capture to return an error.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The PayPal capture flow may already centralize through `finalizeDepositPaymentWithClient()`.
findings.what_would_confirm: Provide the PayPal deposit capture implementation and verify it calls the guarded helper before mutating payment state.

empty_categories: No confirmed mismatch was found in the visible D13 status set: draft and confirmed remain allowed, while cancelled and completed are blocked.
No RLS or permission-model implementation gaps could be confirmed from the supplied excerpt because Group G and the relevant files are absent from the pack.
